name: Chicken Chow Mein #1
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Dried or fresh egg noodles
2 tsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 oz Smithfield ham or prosciutto, finely shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. If using dried noodles, cook according to package instructions, then cool them in cold water until ready to use. If using fresh Chinese noodles, boil them for 3-to-5 minutes, then immerse in cold water.
2. Slice the chicken into shreds that are about 2 inches long using a cleaver or sharp knife.
3. Mix the chicken with 2 teaspoons of light soy sauce and rice wine or sherry in a small bowl. Stir well to combine.
4. Let the chicken marinate at room temperature for about 10 minutes.
5. Heat a wok or large skillet. Add the 1 tablespoon of oil, then the chicken shreds. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes, then transfer to a plate.
6. Clean the wok. Drain the noodles, shaking off as much water as possible.
7. Reheat the pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil and garlic. Stir-fry for 10 seconds, then add the snow peas and ham. Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
8. Add the noodles, granulated sugar, additional teaspoon of soy sauce, and scallions to the wok. Continue stir-frying for about 2 minutes.
9. Return the chicken to the noodle mixture. Continue stir-frying for an additional 3 to 4 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
10. Add the sesame oil and give the mixture a few final stirs before serving on a warm platter.
